title: Palaivananathar Temple
tagline: Palaivananathar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Palaivananathar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Papanasam, in Thanjavur district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: Palaivananathar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Papanasam, in Thanjavur district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshiped as Papanasanathar, and is represented by the lingam. His consort Parvati is depicted as Thavalavennayagal. The presiding deity is revered in the 7th century Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the Nayanars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam.

topPlaces.name: Papanasam taluk
topPlaces.description: Papanasam taluk is a taluk of Thanjavur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Papanasam.

topPlaces.name: Ukkadai
topPlaces.description: Ukkadai is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is the administrative centre of the erstwhile Ukkadai estate.

topPlaces.name: Papanasam
topPlaces.description: Papanasam is a panchayat town in Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located 25 kilometres (16 mi) from Thanjavur and 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Kumbakonam.

topPlaces.name: Gajendra Varadha Temple
topPlaces.description: The Gajendra Varadha Perumal Temple in Thirukkavithalam, a village in the outskirts of Papanasam in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Gajendra Varadha Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Ramamanivalli.

topPlaces.name: Umayalpuram
topPlaces.description: Umayalpuram is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Kabisthalam
topPlaces.description: Kabisthalam is a village under Papanasam taluk in Thanjavur district in Tamil Nadu. Tracing out the Vedic history Thiru Kavithalam was the original name for the place.

topPlaces.name: Sarukkai
topPlaces.description: Sarukkai is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Kabartheeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Kapardeeswarar temple (also called Swetha Vinayagar Temple, Vellai Vinayagar temple, Valanchuzinathar temple) is a Hindu temple situated in the village of Thiruvalanchuzhi (also spelt as Thiruvalanjuli) near Swamimalai in Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshiped as Kapardeeswarar and is represented by the lingam and his consort Parvati is depicted as Brihannayagi.

items.note: Papanasam taluk is a taluk of Thanjavur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Papanasam.
Ukkadai is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is the administrative centre of the erstwhile Ukkadai estate.
Papanasam is a panchayat town in Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located 25 kilometres (16 mi) from Thanjavur and 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Kumbakonam.

The Gajendra Varadha Perumal Temple in Thirukkavithalam, a village in the outskirts of Papanasam in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Gajendra Varadha Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Ramamanivalli.
Umayalpuram is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
Kabisthalam is a village under Papanasam taluk in Thanjavur district in Tamil Nadu. Tracing out the Vedic history Thiru Kavithalam was the original name for the place.
Sarukkai is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

Kapardeeswarar temple (also called Swetha Vinayagar Temple, Vellai Vinayagar temple, Valanchuzinathar temple) is a Hindu temple situated in the village of Thiruvalanchuzhi (also spelt as Thiruvalanjuli) near Swamimalai in Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshiped as Kapardeeswarar and is represented by the lingam and his consort Parvati is depicted as Brihannayagi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Palaivananathar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Palaivananathar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Palaivananathar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (65 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (67 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Palaivananathar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Palaivananathar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Palaivananathar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Palaivananathar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Palaivananathar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Papanasam taluk, Ukkadai, Papanasam, Gajendra Varadha Temple, Umayalpuram.
